content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward methods (ICM and RND) can be repurposed to dynamically allocate working memory capacity for reasoning agents: the intrinsic reward signal that drives exploration to novel unexpected states can instead gate which intermediate reasoning activations are retained in limited working memory, prioritizing uncertain or information-dense chunks that move the agent closer to a solution.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
